channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Considers Escalating Military Actions Against Iran Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: President Donald Trump has indicated he is nearing a decision to escalate military actions against Iran, with the US military planning strikes 'bigger than ever before' as tensions in the Middle East ...

item.title: Nigeria Targets 20 Gold Medals at 2026 Commonwealth Games
item.description: Nigeria is set to make a bold statement at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, aiming for 20 gold medals after a dismal showing in their first participation in 1950. The team, consisting of 72 ath...

item.title: Border Patrol Agent Indicted for Excessive Force After Striking Non-Threatening Suspect
item.description: A Border Patrol agent, Raymundo Ramos Jr., was indicted for allegedly accelerating his vehicle into a suspect who was walking calmly and posed no threat to officers. The incident occurred on January 3...

item.title: Bioethicists debate whether organ retrieval before death should ever be permitted for consenting MAID patients
item.description: A newly published paper in the New England Journal of Medicine has renewed debate over whether organs could ethically be retrieved from patients receiving Medical Assistance in Dying (MAID) before the...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Results: No Jackpot Winners on July 23, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that there were no winners for either of the major lotto jackpot draws on July 23, 2026. The Superlotto 6/49 jackpot prize of P86,718,438.59 ...

item.title: Top 34 Events and Activities in Washington D.C. This Weekend and Next Week
item.description: This weekend and next week, Washington D.C. offers a vibrant array of cultural, social, and culinary experiences for residents and visitors. Highlights include exclusive after-hours events at the Nati...

item.title: Anti-undocumented migrant groups demand stricter immigration enforcement in Cape Town protest
item.description: Anti-undocumented migrant groups marched through Cape Town's CBD on Thursday, demanding stronger immigration enforcement and presenting a memorandum to the Department of Home Affairs. Organisers from ...

item.title: Government and stakeholders collaborate to enhance child protection in Tanzania
item.description: The Tanzanian government has called for stronger collaboration between public institutions, civil society organizations, and communities to address rising cases of child abuse, neglect, and exploitati...

item.title: Australia faces extreme weather risks as El Niño and climate change converge
item.description: The article discusses the growing concerns over Australia's climate crisis, highlighting the potential for an extremely hot winter amid the looming El Niño phenomenon. While political attention has be...

item.title: Political Leadership and Governance Challenges in Modern India
item.description: The article explores the intricate political dynamics between the ruling party and opposition in India, focusing on recent developments that have sparked debates about leadership effectiveness and gov...

item.title: Amy Winehouse's mother reflects on 15th anniversary of her daughter's death and addiction struggles
item.description: Janis Winehouse, the mother of late singer Amy Winehouse, has shared heartfelt reflections on the 15th anniversary of her daughter's death, describing her as 'a victim of her own success.' The article...

item.title: Spain Midfielder Advocates No Punishment for Argentina Players After Post-Match Scuffle
item.description: Tensions escalated following Spain's 1-0 victory over Argentina in the World Cup final, with Argentina's Leandro Paredes grabbing Spain's Eric García by the throat and shoving him to the ground. Pared...

item.title: Legal Verdict in 1984 Case: 4 Women Acquitted, 2 Men Convicted
item.description: The High Court recently delivered a verdict in a long-standing legal case from 1984, acquitting four women while upholding the conviction of two men. The case, which has been under scrutiny for decade...

item.title: Yacht Club Games' 'Mina The Hollower' Soundtrack to Be Released on Vinyl

item.title: Importance of early childhood communication

item.title: Prime Minister urges national unity amid U.S. trade tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney emphasized Canada's strength in unity as he prepared to meet with provincial premiers to address escalating U.S. tariff threats. In a statement ahead of the gathering, Carne...

item.title: Pendlebury defends Quaynor after Crows fans' boos in AFL clash
item.description: Collingwood champion Scott Pendlebury has addressed the backlash faced by teammate Isaac Quaynor after being jeered by Adelaide Oval fans during a crucial AFL match. Quaynor, playing his 150th game, e...

item.title: Tips for Achieving Crispy Fries at Home Like Takeout
item.description: The article provides a detailed guide on replicating restaurant-quality crispy fries at home using simple techniques. Key steps include selecting starchy potatoes such as russets, soaking cut strips i...

item.title: Blake Austin reflects on Wests Tigers' decline after condemning fan behaviour at Leichhardt Oval
item.description: Former NRL player Blake Austin has revisited comments he made earlier in the season after the Wests Tigers' form dramatically declined following their victory over the Canberra Raiders at Leichhardt O...
